themes.user_observation: The user's product features a left sidebar with 8 navigation items that lack visual grouping, making it hard to distinguish between primary and secondary options.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor's sidebar is densely packed with options, which may overwhelm new users despite offering comprehensive navigation.

supporting_reviews.text:
[Reddit Comment on: Have you been frustrated with Trello's UI Recently? Well I made a Chrome Extension that CONVENIENTLY displays your Trello board on the sidebar with all its base features! (Details in comments)]

**Details**

* Board Display - https://iiremember.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/preview_4.png
* Full Page View - https://dashboard.iiremember.com/fullpageview.png
* View Ticket Display - https://dashboard.iiremember.com/sidebar_view_ticket.png

I have no real user base yet so let me know what features you want me to implement (after testing it ofc) and I gladly will lol

[HN Story] (re: Trello celebrates One Million Users)

Trello celebrates One Million Users

[HN Comment] (re: Ask HN: Why is everything changing too fast?)

I too am old, but I also do the same thing. We know that once something that was  simple and great at its job starts being ruined, it's over. It will become more and more bloated and as it starts shedding users it will happen with increasing speed.So, time to find a Trello alternative that is still just a board with columns.

[Google Play Review - 2/5]

I use trello for personal use but I have a complaint, It looks so overwhelming when I open any card and i see all the open options. Trello look can be more minimalistic if in every card all the labels, members, start date and end date in quick actions and that too must be hidden all the time and only visible when in use. So pls do this and I will give it 5 stars...

[HN Comment] (re: Ask HN: How do you handle the information mass HN provides you with?)

By definition, I read stuff and only links that I think will be useful make it into Trello
Also, because the links are categorized well, it removes the need to keep going back and reviewing further etc - I only access the info as and when I need it.The key is in organization & labeling 
It's not just a random pasting of links into the board
I use different lists to categorize broad categories of content & cards as "sub categories" which makes it easier to go back to as and when i need it.It helps that I always have Trello open in a browser so its quick and easy to paste stuff into the board wherever it belongs and move on with my life.

themes.user_observation: The user's product utilizes a kanban board layout with clearly defined columns for task management.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor also employs a classic kanban board style that encourages a left-to-right workflow with distinct task lists.

supporting_reviews.text:
[Reddit Comment on: Trello mobile users: What frustrates you most about using Kanban boards?]

Maybe it already exists somewhere but I couldn’t find it the other day. A search bar would be helpful.

[Google Play Review - 5/5]

Trello really helps me stay organized and manage my tasks easily. The layout is simple and visually clear which kinda mek it aesthetic. I like dragging tasks around to track progress. It’s a very useful app for productivity and planning for me.

[Reddit Post] Trello mobile users: What frustrates you most about using Kanban boards?

Hi everyone! 👋

I’m currently researching ways to improve the **Trello mobile app**, specifically the **Kanban board workflow**. I’d love to hear from you about your experiences:

* What’s the most frustrating part of using Trello’s Kanban boards on mobile?
* Are there any specific features or actions that feel clunky or unintuitive?
* How do you typically use Trello on your phone (e.g., tracking tasks, managing teams)?
* Have you ever switched to a competitor because of Trello’s mobile limitations? If so, why?

Any feedback, big or small, is super helpful. Thanks in advance for sharing your thoughts! 🙏

[Google Play Review - 5/5]

Trello is 📝✅! 😄 Super organized, boards on point, and gets the job done! 👍

themes.user_observation: The user's product includes a prominent 'Add Task' button positioned at the top-right for easy task creation.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor features an interactive '+ Add a card' button at the bottom of each list, facilitating task addition.

supporting_reviews.text:
[Google Play Review - 5/5]

Trello is simple yet powerful for task management. Boards and cards make organization very clear. Great for both personal and team use. Boosts productivity effortlessly.

[Google Play Review - 3/5]

The app is solid and a decent experience overall, but if you want full functionality skip this app and log in through your browser. For example, you can't mirror cards in the app (a big part of my workflow), but it's possible in the mobile browser version. If that's not wholly important to you and you're looking for a Trello experience that has accessible buttons, give the app a try.

[Google Play Review - 1/5]

I use it in my Tablet and I don't recommend using the app here. I've seen how useful it is when I see my friends working with Trello in their COMPUTER, I wonder why is easier in the computer? In my case, I hate the idea of not having the "DELETE" button in the menu when you click in the settings attached to every card, list or board. Developers and owners of Trello, If you want to make our activites easier and help us with your app, please IMPROVE your app to add a simple button that we all need

[Google Play Review - 1/5]

Trello is a good app for productivity if not the best compared to similar apps, but: 1. There is no card button like in the PC version. 2. There is no option to choose which board I would like to show on my widget. 3. There is no due date of checklists (many people need it because in many cases, different deadline is the point of making checklists). Thank you if Trello wants to consider my recommendations.

[Reddit Post] I built a small Blender add-on to integrate Trello (task browsing, review checks, file upload)

In this plugin you can manage Trello tasks without leaving Blender: browse and filter task cards, switch statuses, import attached assets into your scene, automate model checking, and send work for review with files attached.  
You can get more details on Gumroad, if you are interested: [https://polyforge3d.gumroad.com/l/blendertaskmanager](https://polyforge3d.gumroad.com/l/blendertaskmanager)

And I would appreciate if you have any suggestion on task/project management that you think would be useful to have in this add-on.

themes.user_observation: The user's product has a clean layout but struggles with visual hierarchy in the sidebar, making navigation less intuitive.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor maintains a strong visual hierarchy, allowing users to focus on task management while subtly integrating navigation elements.

supporting_reviews.text:
[Google Play Review - 3/5]

I love Trello app. Really. But I have a suggestion. Maybe you should improve the app UI because it looks too... old (?). Like, having more shapes & themes ?? I don't know how to describe it but I hope you understand.

[HN Comment] (re: Visual Task Management for Individuals and Teams)

OK so I don't think I would say, replace Trello right now, but this is really neat service, well done.I especially like how things are visual and how you can zoom in and out and see this as potentially very useful feature. I will continue to review it and use it.Also, describing it as Trello killer, I think made us expect way too much of it. So this might not worked the way it was intended.

[HN Comment] (re: Show HN: The ultimate productivity tool)

It looks powerful but the landing page is overwhelming and hard to read.I can't understand what The Daily Project does from the top texts. The screenshot says it's sort of Trello alternative (btw I would recommend picking more trendy pastel colors, now the design looks a bit dated).The introductions texts are too vague and promotional [1]: "It's more flexible and has the missing features, but keeps the simplicity that we all love." – how does it solve my problems? [2]Then I finally scrolled to the feature grid (most of the visitors will not bother) and it's still not clear how the app works and helps me ("Focus on what's important", "Built for simplicity").The video tour comes too late, a visitor has already left.[1]: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/blah-blah-text-keep-cut-or-...[2]: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/user-centric-language/

themes.user_observation: The user's product lacks onboarding tooltips, which could assist new users in understanding the interface.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor's interface could benefit from tooltips for first-time users, especially for its numerous interactive options.

supporting_reviews.text:
[HN Comment] (re: Trello handed over my personal account to my previous company)

I created a Trello alternative, check it out at https://www.kanrails.comWould love to get feedback!

[Google Play Review - 5/5]

Trello is an outstanding productivity tool that keeps my tasks and projects perfectly organized. The board and card system makes it easy to plan, track progress, and collaborate with others. It’s clean, intuitive, and works smoothly across devices. I love how simple it is to move things around and update details on the go. Whether for personal goals or team projects, it’s reliable and incredibly helpful. 5 stars! ⭐📋

[HN Comment] (re: Easy way to build product tours with Bootstrap)

yeah, for sure.  though, you can't lie that it's a great way to manage tasks. i love the trello UI and i'm sure more people are going to use the trello UX when building task management apps..  thetaboard looks super new.. comments on the card itself? i prefer to keep the cards height minimal, so i'm not a fan of that design choice.otherwise, it seems neat, but i def trust trello more with my data. thetaboard terms and conditions could actually have some content :\edit: clarification.

[Google Play Review - 5/5]

Trello makes managing tasks and projects easy with boards, lists, and cards. It’s simple to use and helps keep everything organized. Great for personal and team use.

[Google Play Review - 5/5]

My experience with Trello has been very productive. The board and card system makes organizing tasks simple and clear. It helps me manage my work efficiently. Very user-friendly.

themes.user_observation: The user's product features a dropdown for client selection, which may confuse new users due to its placement.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor's product has a top navigation bar with multiple view options that could overwhelm new users.

supporting_reviews.text:
[HN Comment] (re: GitHub Projects – Customizable, flexible tool for planning and tracking work)

Big ClickUp user and I feel you comment about it. It has become a massive UX mess. They really need to clean it up per "use case" or have toggles for users to disable UX features.Too much is too much, and it's getting close to being too much on ClickUp.

[HN Story] (re: ClickUp acquires Codegen, forces users into ClickUp to keep using it)

ClickUp acquires Codegen, forces users into ClickUp to keep using it

[HN Comment] (re: Don’t use “click here” as link text (2001))

Am I the only one who is amused by seeing UIs fuss about using “tap” vs “click”? Is there anyone who has ever gotten confused and started looking for a mouse to plug into their phone because something said “click the X button” instead of “tap” it?

[HN Story] (re: Logging into multiple ClickUp accounts and switching between them quickly)

Logging into multiple ClickUp accounts and switching between them quickly

[Reddit Post] We just dropped a full ClickUp 4.0 review

Full Disclosure: I work at ZenPilot & ClickUp Weekly is not sponsored by or affiliated with ClickUp.

We just published an honest review of ClickUp 4.0 covering what's worth your attention, what still needs work, and how to actually get your team through the transition without it turning into months of confusion.

Link to the video: [https://youtu.be/8JwC4EcVTiI?si=2F-J5NPHux-Uzy5M](https://youtu.be/8JwC4EcVTiI?si=2F-J5NPHux-Uzy5M)

Or read the blog post if you prefer: [https://www.zenpilot.com/blog/clickup-4-review](https://www.zenpilot.com/blog/clickup-4-review)

If you find it useful, Gray also runs [ClickUp Weekly](https://youtu.be/ffpFf-XF3qE?si=d6WXQVeKuszIGec-), a show that comes out every week covering what's new in ClickUp and lessons learned from working with teams in the platform. Worth subscribing to if you want to stay on top of things as 4.0 continues to evolve.

And if you have specific ClickUp questions you'd like answered on the show, send them to show@zenpilot.com. We're happy to add them to the docket and cover them in an upcoming episode.

Has anyone else gone through the migration yet? Would love to hear how it's been going for different teams.

themes.user_observation: The user's product presents a clean bar chart for task completion data but lacks data labels and axis titles.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor's product organizes tasks in a clear table format with distinct sections for status, enhancing data visibility.

supporting_reviews.text: ClickUp is a project management tool. All-in-one productivity platform replacing multiple tools. Key features: tasks, docs, whiteboards, dashboards, goals, AI, time_tracking, sprints. Pricing: Free / $7-$12 per user/mo. Target: startups, small_teams, agencies, developers. Competitors: asana, monday, notion.

[HN Comment] (re: GitHub Projects – Customizable, flexible tool for planning and tracking work)

Big ClickUp user and I feel you comment about it. It has become a massive UX mess. They really need to clean it up per "use case" or have toggles for users to disable UX features.Too much is too much, and it's getting close to being too much on ClickUp.

[Reddit Comment on: We just dropped a full ClickUp 4.0 review]

I like the breakdown! My only add is. I think you can adjust views of task due dates Today > Overdue > Future.

Mine are default view Overdue > Today > Future

Plus board view allows to you view tasks in this order.

[HN Comment] (re: Tell HN: Somebody please make a faster task management system)

Another interesting dynamic -- it may hard to tell if some tool is fast enough during early evaluations. I believe clickup was fast when we first tried it, but now that we have lots of tasks in it, it's slowed down. This could just be my poor memory though.

[Reddit Comment on: ClickUp alternatives that are less buggy]

Yeah, ClickUp gets a lot of heat for performance and bugginess. Their development cadence is very aggressive compared to other similar tools so they have a lot of features but personally I think it takes away from core performance and scalability. 

monday and asana are probably the next two most similar tools - of the two, my experience is that monday seems to put a lot more into their performance and scalability. Fewer features I think, but more focus on performance.

themes.user_observation: The user's product buries the export option in a secondary dropdown, making it hard for new users to find.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor's product provides multiple interactive elements but may still leave users searching for specific functionalities.

ClickUp is a project management tool. All-in-one productivity platform replacing multiple tools. Key features: tasks, docs, whiteboards, dashboards, goals, AI, time_tracking, sprints. Pricing: Free / $7-$12 per user/mo. Target: startups, small_teams, agencies, developers. Competitors: asana, monday, notion.

[HN Comment] (re: Contra Wirecutter on the IKEA air purifier)

The issue is why they completely ignore costs and legacy substitution: vendor relations and content.The true answer is often "The old version was just as good or better, don't upgrade."But nobody gets a continual churn of review clicks and affiliate purchase cuts off of that.Ignoring it with "price is hard" is disingenuous on the part of review sites. Notably, this was initially one of Wirecutter's key draws: presenting winners in a price-segmented manner.

[HN Comment] (re: GitHub Projects – Customizable, flexible tool for planning and tracking work)

Big ClickUp user and I feel you comment about it. It has become a massive UX mess. They really need to clean it up per "use case" or have toggles for users to disable UX features.Too much is too much, and it's getting close to being too much on ClickUp.

[Reddit Comment on: Need Help deciding between ClickUp and Motion implementing 119 step Property Onboarding for our Short-Term Rental Property]

Board view will become unwieldy if you create statuses according to your colored lines as “summary” tasks. Totally doable. 

May consider utilizing multiple lists and automated transfers between lists also to keep things organized and still functional. Be sure you check how it lays out in the app before committing to a structure if you intend to use the app down the road.

themes.user_observation: The product includes toggle switches and 'Configure' links that are clearly visible, allowing users to manage integrations easily.
themes.competitor_observation: The competitor's pricing page has prominent call-to-action buttons and a billing toggle that are easily accessible for user interaction.

to get 7 additional days for each month you buy (only for the first purchase, so I recommend you go big from the start and buy the yearly, it's a better value and you get 84 additional days for free). You can also buy the cheapest tier for a year initially to get the extra 3 months, and if you need a higher tier, you can upgrade along the way, it is possible. **IMPORTANT**: When you're on the subscription plans page, scroll down to the bottom and you'll see a field (not the "Coupon Code" field just below the plans, but to the very bottom of the page) where you can enter my code (not to be confused with the "Your referral code is:" part above it) and click **Submit**. Then you can proceed with buying your chosen plan. **ALSO**, if you pay with crypto, you can use coupon code **SIGMA30** in the "Coupon Code" field for a further 30% discount (only for the first purchase if you select the One-Time option).*
   * *To help you choose between Real-Debrid and TorBox:*
      * ***Real-Debrid** is one of the most widely used service, and has probably the largest cache (files already available on their servers and ready to watch) of shows and movies. However, they only allow one connection at a time, meaning that you can't watch on two or more devices simultaneously (you can log in to your Stremio account on as many devices you want, that's unrelated).
